title: Aam Panna
description: A refreshing North Indian summer drink made from raw mangoes, fresh mint, roasted spices, and a sweet-tangy concentrate that keeps well in the refrigerator.
steps: Pressure cook the raw mangoes with the water for 4-5 whistles, or simmer until completely soft.
Cool, peel the mangoes, and scrape all the pulp away from the seed.
Dry roast the cumin seeds, fennel seeds (if using whole), black pepper, and cardamom seeds until fragrant. Grind into a fine powder.
Blend together the mango pulp, ginger, mint leaves, green chili (if using), jaggery or sugar, black salt, regular salt, the roasted spice powder, and lemon juice until completely smooth.
Taste the concentrate and adjust the sweetness, salt, or tanginess. It should taste stronger than you want since it will be diluted.
Transfer the concentrate to a clean jar and refrigerate. It keeps well for about a week.
To serve, add 3-4 tablespoons of concentrate to a glass, fill with cold water, add ice, stir well, and garnish with fresh mint.
notes: For the best flavor, roast and freshly grind the spices instead of using pre-ground powders.
Jaggery gives a deeper, more traditional flavor, while sugar produces a brighter green color.
If the mangoes are extremely sour, increase the sweetener slightly.
The concentrate can also be frozen into ice cubes for quick servings.
For a smoky flavor, roast the raw mangoes over an open flame instead of pressure cooking before extracting the pulp.
